description: Move the durable parts of watchdog/autonomy session state out of browser-local ownership and into project-local runtime files under .bowman/runtime, so the local service can recover and reason about active or recently active workers across reloads and restarts.

reflections.context: Persist durable watchdog session metadata into project-local runtime state under .bowman/runtime so autonomy state is not owned only by the browser or broker-global files.
reflections.outcome: The broker now syncs active sessions into .bowman/runtime/watchdog-sessions.json on start, keepalive, reclaim, and teardown. The frontend also restores from existing broker sessions directly, which reduces dependence on browser-local reconnect flags.
reflections.effectiveness: This worked well as a low-risk first slice because the broker already owned the canonical session list. Mirroring that state into project-local runtime files required small focused changes and immediately improved the architecture.
reflections.lesson: When moving toward durable autonomy state, start by relocating existing canonical state into project-local files before inventing new orchestration. That reduces risk and clarifies the ownership boundary.
reflections.pattern: This matches earlier runtime cleanup work: moving source-of-truth away from browser-local assumptions and toward the local service makes reloads, recovery, and future autonomy features much more coherent.

status.comment: Broker now mirrors active watchdog sessions into .bowman/runtime/watchdog-sessions.json, and the frontend restores existing sessions from broker truth instead of relying on browser-local reconnect intent.
